instruction: Discuss how file descriptor exhaustion relates to this vulnerability scenario.
input: Go before 1.16.12 and 1.17.x before 1.17.5 on UNIX allows write operations to an unintended file or unintended network connection as a consequence of erroneous closing of file descriptor 0 after file-descriptor exhaustion.
output: File descriptor exhaustion occurs when the system runs out of available file descriptors, causing processes to malfunction. In this scenario, it results in file descriptor 0 being closed improperly, allowing unintentional write operations to occur.
